message: The behavior of distinction of empty and null values has changed.
list.description: The default behavior of HDI equals the hdbti setting distinguishEmptyFromNull=true. The processed hdbti file did not specify this setting or set it to false. This could now lead to different behavior of the generated hdbtabledata file if your CSV files contain two subsequent delimEnclosing characters (as specified in your .hdbti or double quotes by default). HDI will now interpret this as an empty string value instead of null. If this is OK for you, you can ignore this warning. If not, please check your CSV files for such cases and remove the delimiters from values that should be interpreted as null.

The behavior of distinction of empty and null values has changed.
WARNING.description: The default behavior of HDI equals the hdbti setting distinguishEmptyFromNull=true. The processed hdbti file did not specify this setting or set it to false. This could now lead to different behavior of the generated hdbtabledata file if your CSV files contain two subsequent delimEnclosing characters (as specified in your .hdbti or double quotes by default). HDI will now interpret this as an empty string value instead of null. If this is OK for you, you can ignore this warning. If not, please check your CSV files for such cases and remove the delimiters from values that should be interpreted as null.

async_warnings: SAP HANA XSJS applications are based on synchronous API. On the other hand, Node.js is an asynchronous runtime. Thus, for XSJS applications that run on Node.js there is a certain code incompatibility. The @sap/async-xsjs module fixes this incompatibility. The async-migrator is used to migrate the code from xsjs to async-xsjs.
Please be aware of the following potential issues while utilizing the async-migrator for XSJS to async-xsjs code migrations:

1. Although the async-migrator is used to migrate a JavaScript project source code to async-await model by replacing sync function calls to async/await statements, the migrator might sometimes miss adding an async/await keyword to a function defination/call. Therefore, developers should thoroughly review and test the converted code for any potential issues.

2. In the async-migrator, the auto Escodegen option is used that automatically determines whether to use single or double quotes for string literals in the generated code. This might sometimes lead to error in the String manipulation functions.

3. Node 20 has been implemented. Consequently, it is critical to update all dependent node modules to versions compatible with Node 20. Any modules not supported must be replaced or removed to ensure optimal functionality.
cap_logs: SAP HANA native artifacts like 'hdbtable', 'hdbcalculationviews' , 'hdbview' and 'hdbfunctions' will not be read by CDS. To make these objects known to CDS, respective proxy files are created with certain limitations. For more information on this, please refer to the Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model Documentation.
These proxy entities are created under db folder with the tag Proxy_*.cds

In calculation views unsupported data types date(), day(), time() and counter datatype ( Integer )  are converted into respective supported data types daydate(), daytime(), secondtime() and counter datatype ( BIGINT ) .
As per the Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model Documentation and SAP HANA Cloud documentation all data types are being converted into SAP HANA Cloud compliant.
While transitioning to CDS, the migration process involves renaming tables. Consequently, all other SAP HANA artifacts that use these tables or views are also updated.
Technical configuration of each entity and comments are converted into CAP compliant using @sql.append statement and doc comments /* */.
Table type definitions are not supported in SAP HANA Cloud. Hence, during the migration, respective .hdbtabletype definitions are created.
capdata: Creating proxy CDS for cross container schema
Following Artifacts are not currently supported '.hdbreptask'
post_mig: Migration of DB Artifacts from XS to CAP is complete. However, please verify these steps post migration:
SQL syntax changes in procedure, ex: UPDATE FROM has to be changed into MERGE INTO, TRUNCATE statement with a DELETE FROM statement.
Reptask and Replication artifacts need to be adjusted to make them CAP compliant.
If Aliases are being used anywhere in the CDS files, please make sure that these are in uppercase.
A folder named 'unsupported_feature' has been created by the extension to contain file extensions that are not supported in SAP HANA Cloud. The files in this folder need to be handled manually.
Unsupported types and functions in calculation view ex: 'CE_FUNCTION', 'CACHE'  etc, please check the SAP HANA Cloud documentation
Verify the cross container artifacts before deployment.
Series entity is not supported in SAP HANA Cloud so they will be removed by the extension. Please check the Migration Documentation for more information.
CDS Access Policy entity definition is not supported in CAP CDS.
For hdbsynonym, hdbsynonymconfig and hdbrole files, please check target object parameters and role name before deployment.
The Memory threshold parameter is not supported in SAP HANA Cloud and will be removed by the extension.
Fulltext indexes are not supported in SAP HANA Cloud and have been removed.
For hdbflowgraph, ensure that the expression content within the node tag is verified before deployment.
For hdbvirtualtable, Please check the Sample Documentation for more information.
